title: Playable Web Game Vertical Slice
summary: A game generation contract that requires mechanics, state, progression, feedback, and production-shaped assets instead of a static themed page.

retrieval.description: Use for browser games, interactive narrative, simulation loops, and playable prototypes.

donorSources.label: Phaser
donorSources.notes: Default donor for real 2D browser game mechanics.

donorSources.label: PixiJS
donorSources.notes: Useful for render-heavy 2D playables and UI-rich scenes.

donorSources.label: Godot web export
donorSources.notes: Full-engine workflow when a Godot-shaped project is acceptable.

prompt: Generate a playable loop first. A themed marketing page is not a game.
Include player input, evolving state, explicit objective, failure or tradeoff, score/progression, and feedback tied to actions.
Assets must serve the mechanics: sprites, backgrounds, audio cues, UI meters, and story panels should map to game states.
If a heavy engine is unavailable, implement a focused canvas or DOM-based vertical slice with the same mechanics.
requiredElements: Input loop with keyboard, pointer, or touch controls
State model for score, resources, level/progression, and failure/retry
At least one mechanic that changes the game state every turn or tick
Audio or visual feedback for success, failure, and progression
A clear finish condition or next-level transition
forbiddenPatterns: static story page pretending to be a game
allowedSubstitutions: Use CSS/DOM sprites if canvas is unavailable.
Use generated SVG or procedural placeholders when bitmap assets are not ready, but keep asset slots named by purpose.
Use Web Audio oscillator cues when licensed audio assets are unavailable.
stackHints: Use Phaser for 2D game loops by default.
Use PixiJS for render-first 2D scenes and custom UI.
Avoid SSR-first frameworks for the game runtime; keep the play loop client-side.

validators.description: The game must respond to player input and update state.
validators.evidence: Playwright flow, keyboard/pointer event trace, and state readout.

validators.description: The game must include score, level, resource, or story progression.
validators.evidence: State snapshot before and after at least two actions.

validators.description: Assets must map to mechanics, states, or story beats and carry provenance.
validators.evidence: Asset manifest with usage, dimensions, and source metadata.

provenance.notes: Seeded after the producer-game experiment exposed that Retrobuilder needs a real playable-game contract.
